input.command:
git add src/extract.ts mod_test.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: normalise HTML fragments before parsing to prevent Readability crash

Readability walks up the DOM via parentNode until it finds a BODY element.
When input is a bare HTML fragment (e.g. Confluence REST API body.export_view),
linkedom returns null at the document root, causing null.tagName to throw.

Wrap any input that doesn't start with <!DOCTYPE or <html> in a minimal
<html><body> shell inside parseDocument() — the single entry point for all
HTML into the pipeline.
EOF
)"

1→# html2md
     2→
     3→Convert HTML to Markdown from the command line or as a Deno/Node library. Built
     4→for feeding web content to LLMs.
     5→
     6→```bash
     7→curl -s https://deno.com/blog/v2.0 | html2md --url https://deno.com/blog/v2.0
     8→```
     9→
    10→Output:
    11→
    12→```markdown
    13→---
    14→title: "Announcing Deno 2 | Deno"
    15→url: "https://deno.com/blog/v2.0"
    16→date: 2026-03-13
    17→description: "Our next major version of Deno..."
    18→---
    19→
    20→## Announcing Deno 2
    21→
    22→The web is humanity's largest software platform...
    23→```
    24→
    25→## Why this exists
    26→
    27→The best way to give an LLM context about a webpage is clean, structured text —
    28→not raw HTML full of `<nav>`, `<script>`, ads, and boilerplate. `html2md` uses
    29→[Mozilla Readability](https://github.com/mozilla/readability) (the same library
    30→behind Firefox Reader View) to extract just the article body, then converts it
    31→to Markdown with full support for tables, code blocks, images, and
    32→strikethrough.
    33→
    34→## Installation
    35→
    36→```bash
    37→# Global CLI (requires Deno)
    38→deno install --global --allow-read --allow-env -n html2md jsr:@dostarora97/html2md/cli.ts
    39→
    40→# As a library
    41→deno add jsr:@dostarora97/html2md
    42→```
    43→
    44→## Library usage
    45→
    46→```ts
    47→import { convert } from "@dostarora97/html2md";
    48→
    49→const html = await fetch("https://example.com").then((r) => r.text());
    50→const { markdown, title } = convert(html, { url: "https://example.com" });
    51→console.log(markdown);
    52→```
    53→
    54→## CLI usage
    55→
    56→```bash
    57→# Reader mode + frontmatter (defaults)
    58→curl -s https://example.com | html2md --url https://example.com
    59→
    60→# Clean text for inline LLM prompts
    61→curl -s https://example.com | html2md --no-frontmatter --strip-links --strip-images
    62→
    63→# Full page when Readability misses the content
    64→curl -s https://app.example.com | html2md --full
    65→```
    66→
    67→## Features
    68→
    69→- **Reader mode** — strips nav, header, footer, ads via Mozilla Readability
    70→- **YAML frontmatter** — title, url, date, description ready for LLM context
    71→- **Full fidelity** — tables, fenced code blocks, strikethrough preserved
    72→- **Flags** — `--full`, `--no-frontmatter`, `--strip-images`, `--strip-links`
    73→- **Pipe-friendly** — reads stdin, writes stdout, zero config
    74→- **Library** — importable from Deno or Node (via JSR npm compat)
    75→
    76→## Documentation
    77→
    78→Full docs are in [`docs/`](docs/README.md). See
    79→[contributing](docs/contributing.md) to set up locally.
    80→

## Comprehensive Exploration Report: html2md

### Current Version & Release Status

**Version:** 0.1.1 (as of 2026-03-13)
**Git Status:** Branch main, 1 commit ahead of origin (unpushed)

**Latest Commits:**

- `bae40df` - ci: revert Deno pin to v2.x to receive patch updates automatically
- `3baa2d9` - ci: pin Deno version to match .mise.toml (2.5.1)

### CHANGELOG [Unreleased]

The unreleased section contains **only one entry**:
```
## [Unreleased]

### Changed
- Refactored internals into `src/` modules (`extract`, `markdown`, `frontmatter`)
```

This was a recent refactor (commit `6ca8122`). The current HEAD commit (`f41221b`) added two new tests but isn't yet in CHANGELOG.

### Codebase Structure

**Total Source Lines:** 370 LOC across 6 files
- `mod.ts` — 157 LOC (library main + JSDoc + types)
- `cli.ts` — 71 LOC (CLI entry point)
- `mod_test.ts` — 142 LOC (16 unit tests)
- `src/extract.ts` — 65 LOC (HTML parsing, meta extraction, Readability)
- `src/markdown.ts` — 40 LOC (Turndown config + custom rules)
- `src/frontmatter.ts` — 16 LOC (YAML block builder)

**Total:** Extremely lean (~370 LOC + tests)

### Code Quality & Type Safety

**No TODOs, FIXMEs, or HACKs found** in any source files.

**Type annotation quirks (intentional):**
- `src/extract.ts` line 38: `as unknown as globalThis.Document` — type bridge between linkedom and Readability (documented in architecture.md as accepted trade-off)
- `src/markdown.ts` line 10: `deno-lint-ignore no-explicit-any` + cast for TurndownService — necessary for npm ESM interop

### Test Coverage

**16 unit tests, all passing** (100% success rate):
- 4 frontmatter tests (title, og:title precedence, description, omission)
- 2 reader mode tests (strips nav/header/footer, keeps content)
- 2 full mode tests (preserves content, handles HTML fragments)
- 2 content feature tests (code blocks, tables)
- 2 image/link tests (kept by default, stripImages works)
- 1 strikethrough test
- 2 HTML fragment resilience tests (NEW as of f41221b)
- 1 result object test

**Coverage:** Runs with `deno task test:coverage` → lcov report + HTML reports in `coverage/`

**Test Gaps Identified:**
1. **No CLI integration tests** — flags are parsed in `cli.ts` but only tested indirectly via `mod_test.ts`
2. **No edge cases for frontmatter escaping** — titles/descriptions with quotes are escaped, but no test for malformed YAML
3. **No error handling tests** — what happens if Readability returns null? Currently falls back to `html` but untested in full mode
4. **No performance/large file tests** — stdin reading works but no test with 10MB+ HTML
5. **No real-world HTML samples** — only synthetic test HTML; no tests with actual web pages

### Dependencies

**Frozen lock file** (`deno.lock`):
- `@std/cli@^1.0.28` — 1.0.28 (JSR)
- `@std/assert@1` — 1.0.19 (JSR, test-only)
- `linkedom@~0.18.12` — 0.18.12 (npm, DOM impl)
- `@mozilla/readability@0.6` — 0.6.0 (npm, reader extraction)
- `turndown@^7.2.2` — 7.2.2 (npm, HTML→MD)

**No type stubs available** for `turndown` — causes `doc:lint` warning (non-blocking, already accepted).

### CI/CD Pipeline

**Workflows:**
1. **ci.yml** — push to main / PR: fmt check → lint → doc:lint → typecheck → test → coverage upload to Codecov
2. **publish.yml** — tag `v*`: runs full CI, then `deno publish` via OIDC (no secrets)
3. **release-drafter.yml** — updates draft release notes on main branch push

**CI Status:** All checks passing (fmt ✓, lint ✓, doc:lint ⚠ turndown type warning, check ✓, test ✓)

**Coverage:** Codecov integration enabled (fail_ci_if_error: false)

### Documentation

**Docs directory structure:**
- `README.md` — quick start, features, installation
- `cli.md` — all flags, examples, exit codes (comprehensive)
- `api.md` — `convert()` function, options, TypeScript types
- `architecture.md` — dependency rationale, data flow diagram
- `contributing.md` — setup, workflow, how to add a flag (step-by-step)
- `publishing.md` — versioning (semver), JSR publish flow, release process

**Doc Quality:** Well-structured, current, and accurate. No stale references post-refactor.

**One gap:** The root `README.md` installation examples are slightly out of sync:
- Shows: `jsr:@dostarora97/html2md/cli.ts` 
- But deno.json JSR publish excludes `cli.ts` directly; correct usage is `@dostarora97/html2md` (defaults to `mod.ts`)
- This is a documentation clarity issue, not a code bug.

### Recent Changes & Quality

**Latest commit (f41221b):**
```typescript
// src/extract.ts parseDocument()
const normalised = /^\s*<(!DOCTYPE|html)/i.test(html)
  ? html
  : `<html><body>${html}</body></html>`;
return parseHTML(normalised).document;
```

This fixes a crash when Readability receives HTML fragments without `<html>` wrapper. Two new tests cover both reader and full modes. This is a solid, defensive fix.

### Notable Design Decisions

1. **Synchronous `convert()`** — all work on in-memory DOM, no I/O; fetching is caller's responsibility
2. **Defaults optimized for LLMs** — reader mode on, frontmatter on, links/images preserved
3. **Readable extraction via Mozilla Readability** — proven, battle-tested (same as Firefox Reader View)
4. **Clean flag semantics** — `--no-frontmatter` instead of `--frontmatter=false`; negatable flags via `@std/cli`
5. **No state or side effects** — pure function; thread-safe

### Rough Edges & Improvement Opportunities

1. **Type safety issue (minor):**
   - `src/markdown.ts` uses `TurndownService as any` because npm ESM export is untyped
   - Workaround exists but is inelegant

2. **Missing CLI unit tests:**
   - `cli.ts` flags are parsed but not directly tested
   - Integration tests would be valuable (e.g., stdin piping, exit codes)

3. **Incomplete frontmatter validation:**
   - Titles/descriptions are only quote-escaped, not fully YAML-safe
   - Complex content with colons, newlines, etc. could break YAML parsing
   - Example: `title: "Title with: colon"` → valid, but `title: "Title\nwith newline"` → invalid YAML

4. **Error handling sparse:**
   - No try/catch around Readability.parse()
   - No validation on URL format
   - No limits on HTML input size (stdin unbounded)

5. **Documentation minor issue:**
   - Installation example in README.md shows JSR path as `/cli.ts` but CLI is not separately published

### Test Coverage Gaps (Specific)

| Scenario | Current | Needed |
|----------|---------|--------|
| CLI flag parsing (--url, --full, etc.) | Indirect via convert() | Direct CLI tests |
| Frontmatter with special chars (quotes, colons, newlines) | Quote-escape only | Comprehensive YAML escaping |
| Readability null/empty result | Falls back to `html`, untested | Test coverage |
| Large HTML (10MB+) | Untested | Stress test |
| Real web pages (not synthetic HTML) | No real samples | Integration samples |
| stdin read error | Not handled | Error case |
| Empty or whitespace-only input | Exit code 1 (tested in CLI) | Unit test in mod_test.ts? |

### Version & Publishing

**Versioning scheme:** Semver (until v1.0.0, minor bumps may break)
- Bump `patch` for bug fixes
- Bump `minor` for new flags/options
- Bump `major` for breaking API changes

**Publishing:** JSR via OIDC (no tokens), provenance enabled, deno.lock is frozen.

### Summary Table

| Aspect | Status | Notes |
|--------|--------|-------|
| **Code Quality** | Excellent | Small, focused, no TODOs |
| **Tests** | Good | 16 tests, all passing, but missing CLI/edge cases |
| **Docs** | Excellent | Comprehensive, current, one minor example issue |
| **CI/CD** | Healthy | fmt✓ lint✓ test✓ coverage→Codecov |
| **Type Safety** | Good | 2 intentional `any` casts, acceptable trade-offs |
| **Performance** | Unknown | Not benchmarked; DOM parsing is fast, but no limits on input |
| **Error Handling** | Minimal | Basic validation, but missing edge cases |
| **Recent Activity** | Active | 1 commit ahead, regular updates, good commit messages |

### What Could Be Done Next

**High Priority (blocks stability):**
1. Add CLI unit tests for flag parsing and exit codes
2. Improve YAML frontmatter escaping for edge-case content
3. Add input size / complexity limits to prevent abuse
4. Test Readability edge cases (null result, empty articles)

**Medium Priority (improves usability):**
5. Add integration tests with real web pages
6. Improve error messages (currently generic or silent)
7. Update README.md installation example to correct JSR path
8. Add a `--no-reader` alias or deprecation note for clarity

**Low Priority (nice-to-have):**
9. Benchmark performance on realistic HTML sizes
10. Document known limitations (e.g., JavaScript-rendered content not supported)
11. Add optional metadata extraction (author, publication date, etc.)
12. Support for custom CSS selectors to override Readability (--selector flag)

**Files of most interest for next work:**
- `/Users/I533118/personal/code/html2md/mod_test.ts` — extend test coverage
- `/Users/I533118/personal/code/html2md/src/frontmatter.ts` — improve YAML escaping
- `/Users/I533118/personal/code/html2md/cli.ts` — add CLI tests + error handling
- `/Users/I533118/personal/code/html2md/docs/README.md` — fix installation example

### Recent Changes & Quality

**Latest commit (f41221b):**
```typescript
// src/extract.ts parseDocument()
const normalised = /^\s*<(!DOCTYPE|html)/i.test(html)
  ? html
  : `<html><body>${html}</body></html>`;
return parseHTML(normalised).document;
```

This fixes a crash when Readability receives HTML fragments without `<html>` wrapper. Two new tests cover both reader and full modes. This is a solid, defensive fix.

### Notable Design Decisions

1. **Synchronous `convert()`** — all work on in-memory DOM, no I/O; fetching is caller's responsibility
2. **Defaults optimized for LLMs** — reader mode on, frontmatter on, links/images preserved
3. **Readable extraction via Mozilla Readability** — proven, battle-tested (same as Firefox Reader View)
4. **Clean flag semantics** — `--no-frontmatter` instead of `--frontmatter=false`; negatable flags via `@std/cli`
5. **No state or side effects** — pure function; thread-safe

### Rough Edges & Improvement Opportunities

1. **Type safety issue (minor):**
   - `src/markdown.ts` uses `TurndownService as any` because npm ESM export is untyped
   - Workaround exists but is inelegant

2. **Missing CLI unit tests:**
   - `cli.ts` flags are parsed but not directly tested
   - Integration tests would be valuable (e.g., stdin piping, exit codes)

3. **Incomplete frontmatter validation:**
   - Titles/descriptions are only quote-escaped, not fully YAML-safe
   - Complex content with colons, newlines, etc. could break YAML parsing
   - Example: `title: "Title with: colon"` → valid, but `title: "Title\nwith newline"` → invalid YAML

4. **Error handling sparse:**
   - No try/catch around Readability.parse()
   - No validation on URL format
   - No limits on HTML input size (stdin unbounded)

5. **Documentation minor issue:**
   - Installation example in README.md shows JSR path as `/cli.ts` but CLI is not separately published
